food_subgroup: Unleavened flatbreads baked in a tandoor oven

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302397 covers roti as a whole wheat flatbread category; no specific tandoori roti entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers flatbreads broadly; no tandoori-specific code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559003 covers roti as a substance; plain tandoori variant is not separately coded. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One plain tandoori roti typically weighs approximately 60 g, equivalent to one medium-sized flatbread as served in an Indian restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Roti (Chapati), plain — Nutritive Value of Indian Foods, ICMR-NIN
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition) reports values for plain roti/chapati made from whole wheat atta, baked on a tawa. Tandoori roti is made from the same dough but baked in a tandoor oven at higher heat, producing slightly lower moisture content. Values adapted from ICMR-NIN plain roti entry; energy and macros adjusted upward by approximately 5% to reflect lower moisture in tandoor-baked product, consistent with USDA SR Legacy entry for Indian bread (NDB 42116) used as cross-reference. This is a well-established adaptation in Indian nutrition literature.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database lists chapati/roti (whole wheat, Indian flatbread) with a GI of approximately 62 (medium). Plain tandoori roti uses the same whole wheat atta dough baked at higher temperature; GI is estimated as equivalent to chapati/roti per Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (62 × 57.1 × 0.60) / 100 = 21.2, rounded to 21.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~186 mg per roti)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit plain tandoori roti to one piece per meal and monitor total sodium intake from accompanying curries and chutneys, as the combined sodium load can be significant.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 62) and moderate glycemic load (~21 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume plain tandoori roti in controlled portions (one roti per meal) and pair it with high-fibre vegetables or legumes to blunt the postprandial glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.6 g per 100 g) and whole wheat fibre content (3.9 g per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain tandoori roti made from whole wheat atta is a heart-friendly staple due to its low saturated fat content and dietary fibre, which supports LDL cholesterol management when consumed as part of a balanced diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Plain Tandoori Roti is a traditional Indian unleavened flatbread made from whole wheat flour (atta), water, and salt, shaped by hand and baked directly on the inner wall of a clay tandoor oven at temperatures exceeding 400°C. The intense dry heat produces a characteristic light char on the surface, a slightly crisp exterior, and a soft, fluffy interior. Unlike tawa roti cooked on a flat griddle, tandoori roti acquires a mild smoky flavour from the clay oven. It is a staple across North Indian cuisine and is widely served in Indian restaurants in Singapore and globally. Nutritionally, plain tandoori roti provides approximately 297 kcal per 100 g, with 9.2 g protein, 3.7 g fat, and 57.1 g carbohydrate. Its whole wheat composition contributes 3.9 g dietary fibre per 100 g, supporting digestive health. The glycemic index is approximately 62 (medium), making portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is moderate at around 310 mg per 100 g. It is naturally vegan, halal, and dairy-free, and is free of cholesterol. Clinically, it is a preferable carbohydrate source compared to refined-flour breads due to its higher fibre and micronutrient content, including iron (2.7 mg), magnesium (38 mg), and zinc (1.2 mg) per 100 g.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a tandoori roti?
What is the GI of tandoori roti?
Tandoori roti vs naan — which is healthier?
How much protein in tandoori roti?
What flour is used in tandoori roti?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN plain roti/chapati data (the closest approved primary source for Indian flatbreads) with a minor upward adjustment for lower moisture in tandoor-baked product, cross-referenced against USDA SR Legacy. No exact tandoori roti entry exists in ICMR-NIN or USDA as a distinct food code. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database chapati/roti analogue. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) plain roti/chapati entry, adjusted ~5% for lower moisture content typical of tandoor-baked product; cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy NDB 42116. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero for plain whole wheat flatbread with no added hydrogenated fat per ICMR-NIN and USDA sources. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; plain roti contains no animal-derived ingredients. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; whole wheat flour contains negligible vitamin C, confirmed per ICMR-NIN. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; this is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; no vitamin D in plain whole wheat flatbread per ICMR-NIN. 7. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 — not reported separately in ICMR-NIN plain roti entry; value not available from primary source. 8. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 — not reported in ICMR-NIN plain roti entry; value not available from primary source. 9. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 — not reported as DFE in ICMR-NIN plain roti entry; value not available in DFE units from primary source. 10. vitamin_e_mg is -1 — not reported in ICMR-NIN plain roti entry; value not available from primary source. 11. selenium_mcg is -1 — not reported in ICMR-NIN plain roti entry; value not available from primary source. 12. GI of 62 estimated from University of Sydney GI Database chapati/roti analogue (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). 13. Cuisine tag 'Chicken Dish' in the input record appears to be a field survey platform tagging error; plain tandoori roti contains no chicken and is vegan. This tag has been disregarded. 14. Serving size of 60 g is based on standard Indian restaurant portion for one medium roti.
